entry.title: Re: CAP Application 404 error after CF deployment
entry.content: Hi, thanks for your support.

entry.title: Re: CAP Application 404 error after CF deployment
entry.content: Hi Thanks for you support.

entry.title: Re: CAP Application 404 error after CF deployment
entry.content:
Hi Thanks for your support. It was possible to reach the service using the /odata/v4/processor to complete the URL. I've changed the xs-app.json file as you suggested but the error persists when I try to access the application through workzone: "Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 ()"

Locally it's working fine

entry.title: Replikation aus dem WM ans ERP
entry.content:
Hallo zusammen,

ich verzweifle langsam. Wir haben ein dezentrales WM im Einsatz. Beide Systeme sind R3 SAP.

Sobald eine Lieferung im WM WA-gebucht wird, wird automatisch das ein IDOC vom Nachrichtentyp SHP_OBDLV_CONFIRM_DECENTRAL erstellt und an das ERP gesendet. 

Meine Frage ist jetzt, ob es eine Möglichkeit gibt auch vor dem Warenausgang eine Replikation ans ERP zu senden, wenn sich zum Beispiel die Liefermenge ändert. 

 

Ich bin für jeden Tipp dankbar. 

Gruß

entry.title: How to trigger workflow by creating a submit form through ui5 application
Re: Get the contracts list whose value is greater than 1...
entry.content:
Hi Bilal,

Thank you for your response, My backend models are based on Live connection and I don't see an option to create account based dimension. Could you please care to explain more on how to create this.

 My SAC details are as below.

Versions
2024.21.15 (Client)
2024.21.14 (Server)
Commits
f9d1d5403a0c37205f1de578d197f1d6c1dc78da (Client)
5bfef6daf9c0af39e1e128ee1a4ecb4590b406a7 (Server)
Build Dates
Wed Jan 22 2025 18:23:57+0000 (Client)
Wed Jan 15 2025 18:43:29+0100 (Server)

Regards,

Rambabu

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @TomHoepping 

I remember you mentioned before that the (now unavailable, but you have an old copy saved) Abap Trial 1909 still runs ok on Docker. And based on the error messages that come with Abap Trial 2022, then the fail-point is indeed about hdbdaemon not being able to get started.

So far, we are in the realm of what we know. Your idea, that it's related to the /proc stuff receiving the wrong arguments with "rosetta/" pre-pended... it's possible, though my feeling is that maybe not probable, as the root cause... because:

1. If you look at the github issue, it was raised in November 2023 and discussed in the months after that. But from summer 2023 till around March 2024, the Abap Trial 2022 on Docker Desktop for Mac, was working fine (including all processes related to hdbdaemon). So the time-periods are incompatible with something in Docker or Rosetta prepending "rosetta/" as the root cause of the issue for Abap Trial 2022.

2. Although it must be something to do with the HANA DB, as that is the main difference between Abap Trial 2022 and Abap Trial 1909 (Sybase DB) in terms of getting the database started (which always comes before any ASCS or CI app server can get started), I am not sure that from the point of view of Rosetta, it makes much difference what the process is... Rosetta is only interested in catching processes, which are trying to perform actions that are x64-specific. ... On the other hand, if you can run Abap Trial 1909 and see whether the /proc is receiving e.g. "sybasedaemon" or "rosetta/sybasedaemon", then that would remove the force of this "Point 2." if it turns out that sybasedaemon is fine because not calling up Rosetta... my guess, which could be wrong, is that Sybase is also doing x64-only stuff and needs to be caught by Rosetta...

But the main reason I think that the root cause is about security tightening for Rosetta, is that a load of issues were reported when, was it, Sonoma 14.4 update rolled out (not just issues for Abap Trial, but also many Java programs in Docker started failing). And those security updates are delivered via OS updates. So whether it is tighter sandboxing of emulated java processes or emulated hdbdaemon process, the results are rather similar, in effect as well as when they started occurring.

So I'm not proud: after all, my hypothesis is also guesswork, not knowledge; if it turns out that your hypothesis is right, and there would be a way to workaround the root cause it points to, then that would be great.

However, if we now do the thought experiment, and say that we assume the root cause is because of the way Apple is parsing the argv0 to /proc... it might be possible, as some suggested in the github thread, to let's say login to a container spun up already from the Abap Trial 2022 image, and do some clever rewrites somewhere, and save them, then restart the container so that, say, in the container, some scripts or utilities or arguments are able to accomodate "rosetta/" additions... might be. I doubt Julie's team would be getting involved in that though, as the idea of the Docker image is that it runs on a Linux VM on any platform... also, as this issue is not actually a showstopper, since for the subset of Abap Trial 2022 users with arm64 MacBooks, there is the UTM/QEMU path still available... so I doubt SAP would be greatly motivated to work on a solution for such a small set of consumers of a (free) SAP product...

Hence my hope is still that in the near future, adoption of arm64 Windows could be a motivation for SAP to provide a Linux arm64 Docker image. In general, many providers of Docker images and even non-containerised apps have started to provide arm64 builds, because in Linux world arm64 is already quite popular - for example, arm64 Ubuntu distros are already quite usable i.e. you can apt install lots of arm64(aarch64) versions of software already.

Sorry this is a bit of a ramble, kinda thinking out loud here 🙂 Have a nice day now, n regards, -dylan

entry.title: Re: Can I backup the S/4 Sap public edition system to an...
entry.content:
Dear Enrique

Thank you for contacting us through our community..

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition does not provide direct mechanisms for backing up data to an external device due to its cloud nature. The data management, backup, and recovery processes are handled by SAP as part of their cloud services.

Below you will find some documents where you can find more details about the backup process for S/4Hana Cloud Public Edition.

Protect Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Security of Data Centers and External Auditing 

KBA 3396152 - FAQs about backup and recovery service for S/4HANA Public Cloud Customers 

I hope it can answer your question.

Kind regards,

Cheerio & thanks
Harry

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 13.0.37 crash in dtsagent.dll
entry.content:
SAP no longer gives out PDB's.

Can you describe the work flow of what is happening when this happens?

Is it reproducible or random?

That file does a lot of things so it could be Memory issues, formatting issues, DB Client issues, etc.

Is it one report, if so can you upload it also? I can run it through my main test app and see if anything shows up.

I worked for SAP for 26 years, retired now...

Is a WEB or Desktop app?

What OS is the app running on?

And anything else to help suggest how to debug the issue.

Don

entry.title: Find out if a component of a structure is a structure or a table type
entry.content:
Hi,

is there any way to describe a structure and than find out if the component is a structure or a table type? Something like this:

    DATA: lo_strucdescr TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr.

    lo_strucdescr ?= cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( cs_ddic ).
    LOOP AT lo_strucdescr->components INTO DATA(ls_components).
        " Here determine if component is a structure or a table type
    ENDLOOP.

Thank you all for help 🙂

Best regards,

Narsi

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part II: Provisioning to BTP and S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Martin

This is definitely huge news for people that have been struggling to integrate SAP with EntraID or IDM solutions. It's really awesome to know that SAP is making an important effort to integrate with standard protocols like SCIM to simplify provisioning.

What I think is missing is the big picture. I understand that this scenario works and someone might want to use for small scenarios, but I'd like to see something more architecture-oriented on these topics like:

    - Concept of "source system". in this case we have EntraID but some SAP customers might want to enrich IAS with attributes coming from other systems like SF. How this should be managed, what limitations do we have?

  - How should we organize this for bigger landscapes having multiple ABAP systems, SaaS solutions, etc. I see this hard to manage an monitor in big landscapes.

- I'm not sure if IAG will be at some point on top of this to play the role of IdM having interfaces to monitor synchronizations, reconcile, etc. IPS is great but is a "low-level" tool that pay the role of integrating but we need something on top to orchestrate the whole process. EntraID can play part of the role but we need some guidelines to organize the processes.

Thanks

Diego

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Assembly list
entry.content: Hello, Does someone knows how to get a list of the assembly with the linked materials?

entry.title: Re: Can we integrate Process manager and Process Collaboration with in Process Governance
entry.content:
When you use SAP Signavio Process Manager for process modeling, you can use SAP Signavio Process Governance to manage process diagram approvals. SAP Signavio Process Manager triggers these process diagram approval workflows, which run in SAP Signavio Process Governance and in turn update the process model in SAP Signavio Process Manager.

The Set model state action automatically updates the diagram state in SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub, for example to mark the diagram as approved or in progress. You typically use this as part of a management approval process that marks the diagram as accepted or rejected.

Can you elaborate on the user case, I am happy to help you further!

entry.title: Re: FLP failed to load catalogs after system copy - what...
entry.content: Unfortunately, there is no general procedure. After a system copy is before a system copy and you would have to create a customer-specific checklist. I would put something together.

entry.title: Re: SAP Private Link service use cases for SAP Cloud Integration and SAP Build Work Zone, Standard E
entry.content:
Hey @MMZ,

BTP endpoints including SAP Cloud Integration are internet-facing by design - like any other SaaS product out there. No way to make that private. SaaS security is about securing app layer not network.

Depending on your scenario you might consider using a timer trigger on your iflow to avoid having an endpoint outside.

KR Martin

Harry

entry.title: Re: Allocation with an # that should be spread - SAC
entry.content: Ok

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 13.0.37 crash in dtsagent.dll
entry.content:
Hello,

It's a desktop app. The error appears on any windows version. The memory dump is for Windows 11.

Apparently I found the problem. We have a windows form which preview the report. In this window it seems that we call CrystalReportViewer.RefreshReport too many times on the load of the form. After I replaced this with something like viewer.ReportSource = viewer.ReportSource the error disappeared. I found that these 2 methods seem to do approximatively the same thing.

Another problem, if viewer.ReportSource = viewer.ReportSource called together with viewer.RefreshReport again breaks the report viewer. If the report has a datasource set into code with SetDataSource(dataset), the report lose this datasource and tries to connect to the database used to design the report. I suppose that RefreshReport does this...

Probably RefreshReport break something if it is called too fast.

I can consider the problem solved, but with this workaround... which is a little weird...

Sorry for my english...

Thank you for the answer.

Gabi

entry.title: Re: Issue in PS Cash Management Planned payment coming fr...
entry.content:
I thought that was the standard behavior of the system but I have the same problem:

Payments committed by invoice with transaction VF01 (value type 54) never reduce the planned payments recorded from the order's billing plan (value type = 62).

This is a problem because the report S_ALR_87013573 shows the summed records and that is incorrect.

Does anyone have a solution?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Flush method is sap mdg fpm

entry.title: How to transfer a paid down payment from sales order 1 to sales order 2?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Let's see the next scenario:

Sales person creates a sales order 1.

A down payment request is created linked to this SO.

This down payment is paid.

The customer will deliver the items based on sales order but some is item is missing.

Final invoice is created and some amount is open from the down payment. The sales person would like to sell another thing to the customer but it means a different business, so he creates a new sales order 2.

The sales order 1 is finished. It was final invoiced, all payments is done.

But there is a remanining amount on the linked down payment. 
How could be linked to the new sales order? Is it possible to transfer it to another sales order?

I have learned that when the final invoice is created from the outbound delivery invoice request then all linked down payment to the original sales order, is automatically take into account in this final invoice.
No way to add another open down payment to the outbound delivery invoice request...

 

Any idea how to solve it?

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: We've recently noticed that Box 13(Retirement Plan Indicator) is not checked for 2024.  Has been checked for previous years and still checks when executing for previous years.  All year-end related notes have been applied.  No changes made to Tax Form Group or Tax Form Fields.  Any ideas what could be causing this?  I've created ticket for SAP to evaluate.

entry.title: Re: CALM & MS Teams Integration changes due to MS O365 co...
entry.content:
@john_p_grimesEscuse me to come back to this topic after that long time.
You wrote "Incoming Webhooks are created per-channel, not per Team .." but in your drawing on "MS Teams" side you named the webhooks for channel A & B (Webhook I) and channel C & D (Webhook II) the same. 
As well as on the destination side of the BTP CALM subaccount.
Is your drawing right or is it a copy&paste mistake?

Thanks for a clarification and best regards,

Dirk

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Enum declaration and accessing values
entry.content:
For future readers: The base type must match with Enum definition

This works:

    types ty_c type c length 8.
    out->write( conv string( ze_testenum-mon ) ).
    out->write( conv ty_c( ze_testenum-mon ) ).

 Output is:

MON <-- Base type doesn't match
1   <-- Base type of conversion matches with Enum defintion

entry.title: Re: Set a fix locale in Plateau Report Designer
entry.content:
I found the answer:
Select

LocTitleEn.label_value as "Fix English Locale of Title"

From

pa_sched a

LEFT OUTER JOIN PA_I18N_LOCALIZED_LABEL LocTitleEn ON LocTitleEn.LABEL_ID = a.schd_desc AND LocTitleEn.LOCALE_ID = 'English'

This works fine, but I get some issues with the dynamic selection of filters when running the report in SF.

I mean: When I run the report in SF LRN and go into the search filters to define which classes I want to see in the report, then this works fine, whenever I eventually display all classes I want and select them. E. G.: I look for classes with title "ABC", display the results, select all found classes and run my report. So, this is basically a static search.

When I only add "ABC" as filter to the search without explicitely selecting the classes (so a dynamic search), the report fails. I have no idea...

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud tenants on Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) updates in 2025
entry.content:
Upcoming enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud for the year 2025 can be accessed through the Road Map page.

Explore the latest features, highlights and updates from our recent releases on the page  https://www.sap.com/products/cloud-analytics/features/release-highlights.html .

If you're seeking information on release dates from the preceding year, 2024, you can refer to the details provided in this Blog Post.

SAP Analytics Cloud aligns with SAP’s global strategy for cloud application releases, following SAP Cloud products 2025 calendar:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Ariba
  • SAP Business Network (incl. SAP Business Network for Procurement, SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration, and SAP Business Network for Logistics)
  • SAP BTP, ABAP environment
  • SAP Business Technology Platform (see exceptions)
  • SAP Concur
  • SAP Customer Experience* (see exceptions)
  • SAP Enterprise Asset Management
  • SAP Fieldglass
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning
  • SAP Manufacturing
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) (see exceptions)
  • SAP SuccessFactors (see exceptions)

*Excludes SAP Commerce Cloud releases

Customer-impacting releases may only take place in February, May, August and November each year.

 

Following each update, upon logging into SAP Analytics Cloud, you will encounter a prominent notification banner featuring a link to our informative "What’s New" help content. You will see Help page of What’s New-2024 Releases.

 

SAP Harmonized Quarterly Updates for 2025 are the following:

  • 2025 Q1: Feb 14-16 (2025.01)
  • 2025 Q2: May 16-18 (2025.08)
  • 2025 Q3: Aug 15-17 (2025.14)
  • 2025 Q4: Nov 14-16 (2025.21)

For more details, see KBA 2888562.

SAP Analytics Cloud QRC release calendar 2025

 

SAP Analytics Cloud, as all cloud products, may have an optional, no-impact release on harmonized monthly feature delivery weekends.

As with the quarterly releases, each product has the option to release during any or all of these windows. Customers should not need to prepare for or take action for the monthly feature deliveries.

 

Note: Content can only be moved between systems on different release cycles at the time of a quarterly release cycle update.

To transport using Content Network: The source and destination systems need to be on the same quarterly version of SAP Analytics Cloud, or the following quarterly version. If the importing system is on an older version, the package won’t appear. If it’s two or more versions ahead of the exporting system, the package’s content may not be supported.

Using the Transport (file) Method (zipped / .TGZ files) – Content can only be imported in the same version of the application from which it was exported, or the subsequent version. After your system is upgraded again, you cannot import the content.

Regards

entry.title: Re: cl_cds_test_environment for nested CDS views
entry.content: But that will not mock the Entity I_BillingDocument (regarding MODIFY ENTITY), only SELECT is available and even that on only the explicitly named CDS. Any CDS selecting from the mocked CDS won't see the mocked data.

entry.title: Free text in automatic payment (F110)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Public Community,

Our requirement is to insert a free text, during the creation of an automatic payment through Manage Automatic Payment app (F110 tcode), that will be reported in the correspondente accounting document generated in SAP as header/item text (NOT in the correspondente DME file).

From my knowledge this is not possible by standard and this is the reason of my question. Have you any possible idea for this?

Best Regards

Liliana Ferreira

entry.title: Re: Is it possible (by rescaling) to avoid section weight...
entry.content: Hi Mridul, thank you for your response. Do you mean there is a way to NOT consider section weights for the overall rating (summary)?

entry.title: Onboarding 1.0 Federal Audit Compliance Report
entry.content:
Hi,   I have a client requirement to create an audit report out of 1.0 to match federal compliance requirements.   I have attached the guidelines/requirements.   This is not a standard report and would need to be built per SAP.   I have not seen this actually done, nor had this type of report actually been requested during any of my I-9 audits while in HR.   

Has anyone created this report?  It looks like it would need to be a combination of Advanced and Signature reporting from 1.0.  

They are moving to a 3rd party 1-9 vendor and want this report for record keeping. 

Thanks, Heather 

 

o   Electronic Form 1-9 Actions Requiring Audit Trail Documentation

§  All electronically generated Form I-9 audit trails must provide the date and time of access to the Form I-9, the identity of the individual accessing the Form 1-9, and the action being taken by that individual on the Form 1-9. This information should be presented in a logical, organized, easily accessible manner.

(i) Retrieve and reproduce (including printing copies on paper, if requested) only the Forms I-9 electronically retained in the electronic storage system and supporting documentation specifically requested by an agency of the United States, along with associated audit trails. Generally, an audit trail is a record showing who has accessed a computer system and the actions performed within or on the computer system during a given period of time;

entry.title: API for Direct FI Customer Invoice
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a requirement to post a Direct FI Customer Invoice in SAP Public Cloud. Our goal is to create the invoice with all essential details, specifically including the HSN Code, Payment Terms, and Tax Code. We have explored API.SAP.COM but have not yet found a suitable API.

Additionally, we tested the Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous) API, which is stated to support postings for Debtors, Creditors, and general journal vouchers. While we were able to achieve most of the requirements, we encountered the following issues:

  • HSN Code, Payment Terms, and Tax Code are not being processed as expected.
  • Although the Tax Code field is available, it is not functioning correctly.

We would greatly appreciate any insights or solutions regarding this issue.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Missing tax number categories of business partner for few countries
entry.content:
How to add missing tax number categories of business partner ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Missing tax number categories of business partner for...
entry.content:
Refer below KBA to add missing tax number categories :

2880427 - How to add missing tax number categories of business partner in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Re: Is it possible (by rescaling) to avoid section weight...
entry.content:
@ellende-paep  You can ignore section weights on calculations in PM Form using the Friendly way. This option is available in Provisioning > Form Template Administration or XML with the attribute rate-by-adding-values="true"

please mark this as answer if this helps

entry.title: How to get all CIV record templates (PDF file)
entry.content:
Customer wants to retrieve all successfully created CIV record templates in the S/4HANA cloud system.

The output result template (PDF file) can be accessed from the "Output Item" tab. However, they want a way to obtain all output templates (PDF files) in one place.

Is there a specific Business Object (BO) for this purpose, or is there an API or OData service that allows to retrieve all output templates (PDF files) at once?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get all CIV record templates (PDF file)
entry.content:
To proceed with any customization related to output items you can follow the documents below:

  • SAP Help - Output Management for Billing Documents
  • SAP Help - Change Billing Documents
  • SAP Help - Manage Billing Documents
  • SAP Help - Sales Billing

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content:
Cool it is useful to be able to search for such content. 

And idea could be also to show the result here. 

 

In Figaf we added similarly concept called tagging that allows you to search via regex/xpath that will allow you to search continues for such problems.

entry.title: Re: CCM Sales Rebates- Posting accruals adjustment instea...
entry.content: Thanks @gvictor for your quick response. The client requires debiting the Accrual Provision (b/S) account at the time of settlement, rather than the accrual expense account. Therefore, a full accrual reversal at the time of settlement is not necessary. Using the account key/G/L account determination, we can debit the Accrual Provision instead of the expense account for the settlement amount. However, my concern is how to get the right accrual at the REA2 condition, as it should have the adjustment amount and not the open accrual amount. The alternatives you suggested are what we have also proposed, wherein no accruals are reversed or adjusted at the time of settlement (both manual and automatic). The business then needs to adjust the accrual manually as applicable. I have a question: Is there any CCM-Settlement transaction to post the manual accruals for the condition contract similar to WB2R_DOC_ENTRY? This posting should also update the WCOCOF table. Thank you for your assistance.

entry.title: Hers material type not present in the system
entry.content:
Hi guru, the hers material is not present in the starter system, does some scope item need to be activated to be present? If so, which one?

 
Thank you so much for your reply.

entry.title: Re: User-exit to redetermine Collection Group in SD-Billing
entry.content:
Hi,

in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition this field belongs to scope item BFC (Collection and Dispute Management). This scope item requires additional license.

When activated you can maintain the field "Collection Group" in business partner (in role UDM000) or in app Manage Collections Master Data. 

There is no option to re-determine the collection group during sales billing.

entry.title: CPI: Groovy[HashMap] Data Retrieval Issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I need to replace all the payScaleArea codes with payScaleArea names for further processing. Considering performance, I don't want to use a looping process call with a splitter as there are more than 5L+ user IDs from CE Query. So I am fetching all the payScaleArea from payScaleArea OData API initiating the HashMap and retrieving it for the target structure.

Step:1 Source XMl will be the body

<queryCompoundEmployeeResponse>
	<CompoundEmployee>
		<id>11111</id>
		<person>
			<person_id_external>ABCDEF</person_id_external>
			<employment_information>
				<action>NO CHANGE</action>
				<last_modified_on>2024-01-13T07:41:27.000Z</last_modified_on>
				<user_id>ABCDEF-1</user_id>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EV</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EM</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EL</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
			</employment_information>
		</person>
		<execution_timestamp>2025-01-30T06:26:34.000Z</execution_timestamp>
		<version_id>1.0</version_id>
	</CompoundEmployee>
</queryCompoundEmployeeResponse>

Step:2 Initiated the HashMap and stored the values in the property

hashmapOutput={ESP/EM\=aaaaaa, ESP/EL\=bbbbb, ESP/EV\=ccccc, ESP/EN\=ddddd}

 Step:3 Target Body

<queryCompoundEmployeeResponse>
	<CompoundEmployee>
		<id>11111</id>
		<person>
			<person_id_external>ABCDEF</person_id_external>
			<employment_information>
				<action>NO CHANGE</action>
				<last_modified_on>2024-01-13T07:41:27.000Z</last_modified_on>
				<user_id>ABCDEF-1</user_id>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EV:ccccc</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EM:aaaaa</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
				<job_information>
					<payScaleArea>ESP/EL:bbbbb</payScaleArea>
				</job_information>
			</employment_information>
		</person>
		<execution_timestamp>2025-01-30T06:26:34.000Z</execution_timestamp>
		<version_id>1.0</version_id>
	</CompoundEmployee>
</queryCompoundEmployeeResponse>

Step:4 Retrieve the HashMap and replace all the payScaleArea values to create the target body above

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {
	
	def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String);
	def map = message.getProperties();
	def hmap1 = map.get("hashmapOutput");   
   	
   	def Root = new XmlParser().parseText(body);
    Root.CompoundEmployee.each{CE ->
	    CE.person.employment_information.each{EMP->
		    EMP.job_information.each{JOB->
		     if (hmap1.containsKey(JOB.payScaleArea)){
		         JOB.payScaleArea[0].value = hmap1.get(JOB.payScaleArea[0].text().toString())
		     }
		    }
		    }
		}
       
	message.setBody(XmlUtil.serialize(Root));
   	return message;
}

The values itself is not getting replaced. 

Any leads will be invaluable.

Regards,

Pavan

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cloud Integration Code search tool
entry.content: Nice blog Fatima. Thanks for sharing with community.

entry.title: Transfer between warehouses in the same plant in public cloud
entry.content:
Hi guru,

In public cloud is the transfer between warehouses in the same plant managed, with the delivery? which scope object should be implemented?

Thanks for the reply

entry.title: Re: CPI: Groovy[HashMap] Data Retrieval Issue
entry.content: This can be done quite easily with a simple XSLT.

entry.title: Risk points for remove stage version content catalog
entry.content: Any risk to maintain only online content catalog and removed the staged content catalog version.?

entry.title: Generate PDF Form binary with script in SDK
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

 

In SAP Cloud Applications Studio, I am trying to generate a pdf form with this function:

var formTemplateCode : OutputRequestFormTemplateCode;
formTemplateCode.content = "ME_08";
var formTemplateLanguage = "E";
var binary :BinaryObject;

binary = OutputManagementUtilities.GetPDF(this, formTemplateCode, formTemplateLanguage);

The code is in the Root-AfterModify.absl of an OutboundDelivery business object extension and I am trying to generate a Delivery Note form.

When testing with debug, we are going through those lines but the result of "binary" always is empty.

I have read the documentation of "OutputDocumentUtilities" and it seems to be usable in the SDK for SAP ByDesign.

 

Do someone has any ideas of hat can be the problem here?

 

Thank you for your help,

Marion Marolany

entry.title: Implementing 1SG – Group Reporting: Missing Clear Setup Instructions
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

We are currently implementing 1SG – Group Reporting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, but I am struggling with the lack of clear instructions on how to properly set it up and test it. The available test script does not explain everything, and KBA Note 2659656 (https://me.sap.com/notes/2659656/E) is also somewhat unclear—it doesn’t provide a structured guide on the complete setup process.

Some of my key questions:

  1. Is there a clear, structured document or guide on setting up 1SG from scratch and testing if it works correctly? A step-by-step explanation covering both configuration and validation would be extremely helpful.
  2. What is the correct sequence for setting up 1SG? There are multiple moving parts, like ACDOCA data extraction, ACDOCU mappings, eliminations, and reporting. Is there a defined process to follow?
  3. How does the data flow from ACDOCA to ACDOCU in this process? What are the key configuration steps to ensure data is transferred correctly?
  4. How are intercompany eliminations handled? For example, if Company A sells to Company B within the group, how does SAP ensure revenue is not double-counted in the consolidated financial statements?
  5. Is it possible to renumber the SAP pre-delivered FS (Financial Statement) Items? If yes, what is the correct approach?
  6. Are there any additional KBAs, SAP Learning Hub materials, or best practices that provide better clarity on 1SG implementation?

Since I am handling this implementation alone, I want to ensure I configure and test it correctly. Any insights, guides, or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Accept API payment through External Cash Desk via SAP BTP
entry.content:
Thanks Amlan for your response.

I raised SAP DPAO to my client, but they don't want to spend more. Hence, going with external cash desk.
Thanks for your all support as always 🙂

entry.title: Automatic Assignment of Responsible Release Execution to Workflow Tasks
entry.content:
Hi All ,

I have requirement to add change responsible person as workflow task user in the process route and send notification to the my inbox app.
Can someone help here how to enhance the application.

There are always different persons responsible for the Release Management process
in order to execute the workflow tasks within the release process. As of now, a Team
called Placeholder was used which is needs to be  replaced with an SAP-User who is the actual agent of the Workflow Task. 

We have created Background Services can be defined for the Change Record, which can be added to the Workflow Templates and they will be automatically executed by the system.

entry.title: liveCache Applications plugin in S4HANA
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We are using the RISE PCE environment, and the Business team wants to use capacity planning apps that require a Live Cache Application Plugin on the HANA component.

Is it generally allowed to install Live Cache on the HANA RISE environment, or would it require an additional cost/license?


Refer : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/21aead0c98bd4755abdacd91c99e3393/96592cfe7187429c9a69fda4e6976c50.html?version=2023.001

Prerequisites for Capacity Planning Apps:

Before you start working with the apps, make sure that the following criteria are met:

Use the right formulas to generate the capacity requirements of the work centers.

Set the work center capacity to finite scheduling to get the capacity requirements of the orders.

Install SAP liveCache (which is the HANA component SAP LCA (also called LCAPPS- or liveCache Applications plugin) for Capacity Scheduling Table and Capacity Scheduling Board apps.

Best Regrads,
Rattasak

entry.title: Profit Center and Cost Center Groups - Where are they used?
entry.content:
Hello fellow SAP consultants,

I have a question regarding the practical use of profit center groups and cost center groups in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. While I understand that they help with structuring and reporting, I need specific use cases and the exact Fiori applications where they are actively utilized.

For example, one use case I’m aware of is in the "Manage Allocations" app, where cost center groups can be used to define sender/receiver rules. But where else are these groupings leveraged in real business processes?

I am particularly looking for:

  • Allocation & Distribution – Besides "Manage Allocations," which other apps use them?
  • Reporting & Analysis – Which Fiori reports allow filtering or selection by these groups?
  • Master Data Governance – Are they used in workflows or approval processes?
  • Other Processes? – Any use cases in planning, budgeting, or intercompany scenarios?

Please share the names of the exact Fiori applications where you have used profit center or cost center groups. This is key for understanding their practical application.

Looking forward to your insights!

entry.title: Re: CPI: Groovy[HashMap] Data Retrieval Issue
entry.content: Incoming payload is from compound employee query, and there are multiple payscale area, group, type that needs to be lookedup on multiple times, so I am going with Hashmap. Earlier[https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cpi-groovy-hashmap-data-retrieval-issue/qaq-p/12569463] I faced a similar challenge and fixed, but the same isn't working now.

entry.title: Create records in same session with two separate MODIFY calls on BO using RAP but only 1st call pass
entry.content:
I am trying to create records in same session by calling modify on the BO (using RAP internally). THe first modify call passes and creates the record but second modify call fails during COMMIT (there were no failed keys during second Modify) . 

Exception: CX_ABAP_BEHV_COMMIT_FAILED

 

THere is no entry in Failed Entry after Modify but COMMIT work gives error. 

ALso if i try to call modify of the same data in separate session, it passes. Why is there an issue with back to back create of different entries in same session?

entry.title: Re: CCM Sales Rebates- Posting accruals adjustment instea...
entry.content:
Hi @hemants2 ,

Unfortunately, that approach does not align with SAP CCM best practices.

While it is technically possible to manipulate the system—such as adjusting the Account Key to post to the Balance Sheet and configuring the system to prevent automatic accrual reversal—I strongly recommend reviewing and aligning the FI posting process with standard practices. Deviating from best practices could introduce additional manual effort and increase the risk of errors in financial postings.

Regarding your other question, there is a standard settlement document type available for manual Delta Accruals:
0S4B – Sales Rebate Manual Delta Accruals

Regards,
Gauthier Victor

entry.title: Re: AGING REPORT FOR ACCOUNTS PAYABLE IS NOT WORKING IN S...
entry.content: Yes @MEntrup, just need to update access of SAC in IAS for the embedded analytics.

entry.title: Sospensione operazione di un ordine di produzione
entry.content:
Salve, è possibile sospendere un'operazione di un ordine di produzione per un determinato periodo di tempo?
se sì, come si fa?

entry.title: Re: Create a report with the result of the transports imported using SAP CTMS
entry.content:
Dear Javier,

Thanks a lot for your support here! I could also already talk with the colleagues from development and they could share some more insights:

  • First, as you already realized, is the cTMS API not intended for reporting, as requirements do differ per use case, so a flexible data model or "data tool" would be required (such as SAP Analytics Cloud).
  • Concerning your issues with adding an OR clause - here, please try to use comma instead of 'OR', e.g. status=in,re,ru,fa,su,wa,er,de
    Apart from that, you did everything right also from the colleague's point of view.
  • Although the colleagues do work on improved APIs for export/import/discovery, there would be no improvements planned on APIs that would help you directly with this use case - therefore, please feel free to propose corresponding improvements via SAP Customer Influence.

Thanks a lot again,
Borist

entry.title: Re: How to Add a Nested JSON Object in RestService.SendRequest Action Body in SAP MDK?
entry.content: Are you using the Send Request Action or calling send request from a rule? Can you share the metadata of the rule / action?

entry.title: Ariba : Activated : Purchase Order API
entry.content:
Dear All, 

We have business requirement to call Ariba Purchase Order API  ( <system>/api/order/v1.0/prod/orders )  to read data from Ariba system via our Interfaces. 

I have checked in Ariba Developer portal unfortunately this API doesn't available in our system. Could you please let me know how to activate the above API in our system. 

Thank You so much

Best Regards

Sri

entry.title: Re: Capturing Delta Requirements: A Guide to Fit-to-Standard Workshops
entry.content:
Thank you, Rajen, for this detailed and practical guide!

Your insights on structuring workshops, defining business process hierarchies, and categorizing delta requirements provide a clear roadmap for organizations moving to SAP S/4HANA. The focus on simplification, business alignment, and reducing complexity will help teams create effective and future-ready processes.

This blog is a practical resource for ensuring a structured, business-driven approach to defining to-be processes while keeping transformation projects on track, efficient, and future-proof.

Looking forward to the community’s thoughts—how have your Fit-to-Standard workshops shaped your transformation journey? Let’s discuss it! 🚀

entry.title: Re: SAP User Experience Update: What’s New for SAP S/4HANA 2023 (Private Cloud and On-Premise)
entry.content:
Hi @FrankKrause,

We do provide an API class /UI2/CL_FLP_SETTINGS_API which allows you to write entries which are not connected to the transport system. In the maintenance UI these settings are shown as "Origin = Setup". You could call this API via a small Z-report for example.

entry.title: PowerBi x Sap Enable Now - Lesson Detail Report for alle projects
entry.content:
Hi all! A question regarding the connection PowerBi x Sap Enable Now 

I have followed the instructions to connect reporting data of my Manager to Power BI (Infocenter --> Product integration --> Connecting 3rd party analytic applications - https://enable-now.sap.com/ic/wa/ext/~tag/published/index.html?show=group!GR_F8BA0FC968CD2EB9#group!GR_C7FFC231FB636699). Everything works perfectly.

In the documentation about Server Data Endpoints (Infocenter --> Product integration --> Connecting 3rd party analytic applications: note for experts - https://enable-now.sap.com/ic/wa/ext/~tag/published/index.html?show=group!GR_F8BA0FC968CD2EB9#group!GR_C7FFC231FB636699) I found how to connect to a "Lesson Detail Report" of a specific recording/project. Also works perfectly.

However, I would like the data of the "Lesson Detail Report" for all recordings ("projects") in my workarea, specifically of the practice mode ("uebung").

This is the set up: [your_manager_url]/wa/[workspace_id]/[object_type]/[object_uid]/[mode]/lessondetailreport

I have tried using [object_uid] = uid of the root group but that does not work.

The Power BI template "Workspace Analysis" (also found in the Infocenter) contains the Lesson Detail Report of all projects! But I can't reproduce the way that they constructed it...

How do I get the Lesson Detail Report for all projects?

entry.title: Re: Templates on Disclosure Management
entry.content:
Hi Iwona,

Thank you for your question.

This question is not so much related to headers/footers (what this blog is about), but the handling of this in iXBRL.

There might not have been a well-known case for headers in the incidents for ST2200 so I would recommend opening a Support case, as it would be needed files, logs, etc. which cannot be exchange on SCN.


Please notice that:

Before creating the case

  1. Respect case's priorities, as described in SAP Note 67739 - Notes - SAP for Me Please make sure that the issue fulfills the requirements for the selected priority, especially in case of Very High priority.
  2. Please make sure to select the correct component for your case. Creating the case in the wrong component can lead to increased processing times.

Issue Description

Create a step by step description with screenshots and comments about the issue. Mention all the apps, user data, the actual result and the expected behaviour from the system.
Do not attach individual screenshots one by one to the case, instead use Microsoft Word or Excel to put your screenshots into a single file.
This file is one of the most important items of this list. It will also make sure that we are looking at the correct issue and following the correct steps, specially when it's an issue in a productive system.

entry.title: Monitor heartbeat of non-SAP systems/services
entry.content:
Hi,

A common scenario in Health monitoring, is to check if a web service (or cloud system) is up and running.

However our team hasn't been able to find a way to configure this basic scenario in ALM if the service/system is non-SAP.

The only thing that we've found is:

  • Using ALM synthetic user monitoring to create a Selenium script. (Which requires a Selenium server)
  • Using the Inbound Logs API (Which requires the non-SAP service to be able to send messages to ALM)

Seems rare that simple connection test + alerting is not part of ALM features. Are we missing something?

SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: Issue with Purchase Requisition Release Strategy (App...
entry.content: Set which confirmation codes users can confirm from the authorization object. The problem will be solved.

entry.title: Re: API for Direct FI Customer Invoice
entry.content:
Did you use Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous) 

Can you elaborate on 'not being processed as expected' and 'not functioning correctly' 🤔

entry.title: Item is not displayed on the user's Learning plan if it's assigned or registered on the same date
entry.content:
Hello Learning colleagues,

we have following process: ILT is created with multiple classes available. Users can register to several classes

Issue description: 

1) User registers to several classes in advance (e.g. x, y, z) 

2) User completes class x training. Instructor records attendance. Administrator records completion (Add Learning History with Finance)

==> the Item then disappears from the user's learning plan even though he is still enrolled to y and z

KBA 2543971 is acknowledging this behavior. But I would like to know how you mitigated this behaviour if you came across this issue. Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP BNAC and SAP BPA: Integration and Process Design
entry.content:
Following this blog article, you will configure a SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC) instance and integrate SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) via the provided BPA interfaces. In order to follow the relevant steps outlined below, please make sure to meet the following prerequisites.

Prerequisites

BTP Subaccount Entitlements

  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)
  • SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)
  • (Optional) SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Instances & Subscriptions

  • SAP Asset Intelligent Network, standard edition (until the time of this writing)
  • Destination Service, lite
  • SAP Build Process Automation, standard edition
  • (Optional) SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Cloud Foundry Runtime

  • At least on CF space for service deployment (e.g. dev)

Setup

Assuming the scenario, the services and instances mentioned above are deployed on a new BTP subaccount. In this case, additional configurations must be made to ensure proper functioning of the applications.

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration (BNAC)

Beginning with the SAP BNAC application. After initial creation of subscription and the according instance, BNAC requires a so called "object prefix", which is used for populating the underlying database schema. In order to set this prefix, go to Application Settings > General Application Settings and set the Object Prefix within the dedicated input field.

Optional: Due to its purpose as network application, BNAC allows the sharing of business objects within a certain group of business partners. To invite a business partner, please go to the application Business Partners on the BNAC dashboard and search for the relevant business partners using the search options. Nevertheless, this step is only necessary in cases you want to share and receive business objects from other partners.

After taking these steps, the BNAC instance could be used to create and configure business objects.

SAP Build Process Automation (BPA)

The tool suite and integrations offered by SAP BPA is not only restricted to execute processes in an automated manner. Beside the possibility to connect mail servers for sending pre-defined e-mails, approval forms could be send via "My Inbox" in SAP Build Work Zone and so called agents allow offloading dedicated tasks from BPA.

For using the standard process steps offered by BPA, not all of these integrations are necessary. Due to the intention of this article to give you an overview of the integration possibilities, the required setup steps are described in the next sections. Feel free to choose the capabilities you want to use. Otherwise you could skip the next parts.

Mail Server

SAP BPA offers the possibility to send e-mails during the execution of a process. These mails differ into:

  • free form e-mails, received in the mailbox of your choice
  • approval forms, received within the inbox of SAP BPA (which requires SAP Work Zone)

To send mails in general, a mail server is required. Setting up this mail server and the corresponding mail address, which will be used by SAP BPA, the administrator must create a Destination called sap_process_automation_mail in the subaccount BPA lives in. This could be done within the BTP Cockpit in section Connectivity > Destinations. An example configuration to connect a GMAIL account with SAP BPA follows below: 

 

# Exported BTP Destination
mail.smtp.port=587
mail.bpm.send.disabled=false
mail.user=<YOUR-MAIL-ADDRESS>
mail.password=<YOUR-PASSWORD> or <CLIENT-CREDENTIAL>
mail.smtp.auth=true
mail.smtp.from=<YOUR-MAIL-ADDRESS>
mail.smtp.starttls.enable=true
mail.smtp.host=smtp.gmail.com
Name=sap_process_automation_mail
mail.smtp.ssl.enable=false
Type=MAIL
mail.smtp.ssl.checkserveridentity=false
mail.transport.protocol=smtp
Authentication=BasicAuthentication
mail.smtp.starttls.required=true
mail.description=Choose your own
ProxyType=Internet

 

After creating this Destination, SAP BPA should automatically source the variable and establish the connection to the defined SMTP server. The resulting configuration shown in Control Tower > Mail Server should look like this:

Overview of mail servers found by search for the configured Destination variable

Agent

Before the process modeller could use the Automation library, an agent needs to be configured within the used environment. In order to create such an agent, the subaccount administrator have to make sure, that the subaccount has the entitlement of automation-unattended with a minimum amount of 1 as part of process-automation entitlement. This allows the agent to execute dedicated tasks in the unattended mode. For further information about the topic of agents, please feel free to visit SAP Help Agent Documentation.

To connect an agent with SAP BPA, the agent software needs to be installed on a system connected to SAP BPA. During the creation process of this blog post, a local workstation (MacBook Pro) was utilised. To use this kind of automation within more productive settings, keep in mind that you could install the agent software on servers as well.

First of all, a technical user is required, which will later be assigned to the agent and serves as representation in front of the system. Therefore, visit the Repository-Based Shipment Channel (RBSC) and create a user. After choosing a name satisfying the naming conventions, click on the user within the user list and search for the item Strong Cryptography Authentication in the appeared listing. Click on the option Generate JWT and store the generated secret in a safe place for later usage.

Next, go to the Control Tower section within SAP BPA and go to the section Agent Configuration containing the Agents application. After a fresh install, the list of available agents should be empty. To change this, click on Go to Download Page in the top right corner. This leads to a page, which allows you to download the software packages required for installing the agent on a system of choice. Beside downloading the software, this page is an indirect installation instruction guiding you through the process:

  1. Install the Agent
    After downloading and installing the agent, you should see an icon as part of your title bar on your system. This ensures, that the installation process worked.
  2. Allow Extension
    Please close now all your browser instances and restart your browser. This will initiate the setup of the browser extension used by SAP BPA to debug processes with agents involved (in case you want the extension in private browsing windows as well, please configure this within the extension settings in your browser).
  3. Register
    Return to the page inside your browser and click on the *Register Agent* button. This will initiate the last step of the registration process within the agent on your local machine.

Optional: To manually register an agent with your SAP BPA instance, the Agent Configuration > Agents overview in the Control Tower offers the Register Agent button (beside the Go to Download Page button). Use the presented link and switch to your local agent. After clicking on the agent icon, a small window with all configuration options should appear. Here, click on the cloud icon in the bottom left corner and now on Add Tenant. Please paste the link from SAP BPA here.

Tenant configuration using the provided URL for manual registering

 

Configured agent connected to SAP BPA

After following the previous steps, the agent should be ready for usage in an environment of your choice. To finalise the agent setup, the agent must be assigned to an SAP BPA environment. Therefore, switch to Control Tower > Tenant Configuration > Environments. Select the preferred environment from the previewed listing (e.g. the default Public environment). In the environment overview page, select the tab Agent Management. Within this tab, use the button Add Agent in the top right corner and select your registered agent. Finally, you should have one agent listed within your environment.

Overview of configured agents linked to SAP BPA

Good to know: In order to utilise your configured agents, please make sure, that the version of the registered agent is equal or greater than the specified agent version in your processes.

Destinations

In order to call web service APIs, Destinations created on a subaccount level must be created upfront. To source their endpoint specific configuration (e.g. authentication, header information, ...) within a processes, SAP BPA requires an additional step to map internal Destination Variables onto BTP Subaccount Destinations. Following the principle of the previous integrations, the mapping process for Destinations within SAP BPA is done via the Control Tower. Arrived in this part of the application, select the Destinations tile as part of the section Backend Configuration.

Please make sure, you have created and configured the Destinations (BTP Subaccount level) you want to use for the mapping process. Otherwise, BPA may not find the entries and cannot add them to the instance. Assuming, the Destinations are correctly created and saved, click on the Add button beside Open in BTP Cockpit and select the Destinations pointing to the systems you want to contact. Furthermore, associate the desired environment (or per default associate them with all environments) as part of the next step and then click Add Destination. Now you should see the created *Destination* as part of the listing.

Mapped Destinations from BTP Subaccount

BNAC - BPA Integration

After the individual configuration of SAP BNAC and SAP BPA, this section guides you through the process of connecting both worlds with each other. Therefore, the first step is the creation of another Destination in the BTP subaccount.

Prerequisite: Make sure you have created a service key for SAP Build Process Automation instance within the BTP Cockpit. This information is used now for connecting both systems. The required details from the service key are indicated by the placeholder values used in the Destination template below.

 

# Exported BTP Destination
content-endpoint=https\://<SAP-BPA-URL>/workflow/rest
Type=HTTP
Description=Choose your own
User=<CLIENT-ID>
Password=<CLIENT-SECRET>
Authentication=BasicAuthentication
ProxyType=Internet
URL=https\://<SAP-BPA-UAA-URL>.hana.ondemand.com
Name=ac_workflow

 

The dedicated keys of the service key are outlined in the provided reference below.

Reference:

  • <SAP-BPA-URL>: Value could be found in "endpoints"."api" in the service key. Please do not forget to add the suffix '/workflow/rest'
  • <SAP-BPA-UAA-URL>: Value could be found in "uaa"."url" in the service key
  • <CLIENT-ID>: Value could be found in "uaa"."clientid" in the service key
  • <CLIENT-SECRET>: Value could be found in "uaa"."clientsecret" in the service key

Similar to the integration of a mail server within SAP BPA, SAP BNAC identifies this Destination automatically as a SAP BPA endpoint for starting mapped workflows after certain events are triggered. To map a workflow to an event type, use the Manage Workflow application accessible via the BNAC dashboard within the Administration section. The final result should be similar to the example provided afterwards, demonstrating a process mapped for the Create Equipment event type. This will trigger the mapped process each time an equipment is created within BNAC.

Example mapping of SAP BPA process to a "Create Equipment" event from SAP BNAC

Creating a Process

Based on the configurations made in the previous chapter, SAP BPA is capable of creating processes acting on the behalf of SAP BNAC and further process information without the attendance of a human user. As part of the underlying scenario, a process was created, which could be used to automate the sharing of business objects, equipments in this case, with already connected business partners.
A complete overview of the process, is shown in the Example process for sharing and publishing an equipmentattached picture. Basically, the process listens via an API Trigger on incoming requests from SAP BNAC after an equipment is created. Afterwards, the process queries SAP BNAC for further information about the equipment created using an Action. This information will be used to create an approval form, which requests confirmation by a specified person. After the person approves the received form request, the process uses the SAP BNAC API to publish the specified equipment and share it with a connected business partner. Finally, a free form e-mail is send out, that the sharing process succeeded. In case of an rejection, the specified persons are informed about the send out approval was rejected.

Process Trigger

By using SAP BNAC as the trigger instance for the modelled processes, the first event must be of type API Trigger. As defined defined in the Destination ac_workflow, created in the previous section, SAP BNAC calls this URL after the specified event type is fired. Beside solely calling the URL, SAP BNAC provides further information to SAP BPA. This includes the process which should be triggered combined with a context. The context describes some HTTP payload carried by the request, which could be used as process input. In case you want to dig deeper into this mechanism, please take a look at  this SAP Blog article.

SAP BPA offers, apart from the API Trigger, additional trigger types, which could be used. Nevertheless, for linking SAP BNAC with SAP BPA, you have to use the above mentioned type. Otherwise, the process will not start.

Process Input

After defining the trigger for the new process, process inputs must be defined. In this case, it is data coming from the calling application (BNAC) and which will be used in the subsequent steps. As already mentioned in the previous section, the incoming request carries some context containing certain information. By taking a closer look at the incoming request, the following context could be identified.

 

{
  ...
  "context": {
    "objectid": "<EQUIPMENT-ID>",
    "type": "<TYPE>",
    ...
  }
}

 

Input variables derived from the context send from SAP BNAC

Beside an objectid representing the business object, a type is specified. For this scenario, the process only requires the objectid to request additional information about the business object at the beginning of the process. To make this information accessible withinthe process flow, the configuration of process variables is necessary. Therefore, open the Process Details view (use the << button on the right hand side of the screen) and switch to the tab Variables. Now, click on the Configure button within the first section Process Inputs and provide the desired information. An example configuration for the provided context from BNAC is shown afterwards.

With this input variables in place, the process modeller could access this information throughout the whole process as an input for Actions, Forms, Approvals and Controls.

Process Modelling

To achieve the desired outcome, the process flow and the required process steps may differ from case to case. Nevertheless, this section shortly introduces some common steps which demand further configuration to work (e.g. Action, Automation).

Action Steps

By creating an Action, a library of HTTP requests will be created, which could be included into a process as a dependency. Like other artefacts in SAP BPA (e.g. SDK, Process), they could be published, shared and used by other SAP BPA users. As part of this section, the process of creating an Action calling the Equipment API of SAP BNAC is briefly explained. These steps could be adapted and adjusted for the requirements of the API to call.

At the beginning stands the creation of an Action via the SAP Build dashboard. To create a library, please go to the Connectors section in the side panel of BPA and select Actions. After an initial setup, the list of available actions should be empty. Now, click on Create in the top right corner. For the purposes of this use case, the built actions are configured from scratch. Therefore, choose the option Build from Scratch in the Choose an API Source popup. Provide a meaningful name and a description for the Action you want to create and click on Create. As a naming convention, the domain of the Action could be used (e.g. EquipmentActions similar to this case).

If everything worked out, a new browser tab will be opened and a tile to create the first HTTP request should appear. Based on the underlying scenario, let's create a request to retrieve further information about the created equipment using its identifier (via the objectid input variable):

  • Operation: GET
  • Name: Get Equipment Header
  • Description: Get the header information for a specified equipment
  • Endpoint: /equipment({id})/header

Now click on the Add button. You should be presented with a GET request with no parameters as input and no output. To specify an input, like indicated via {id} in the endpoint, click above the Parameter table on Add, choose New Field and provide the following configurations:

  • Key: id
  • Mandatory: YES
  • Parameter: path
  • Type: string
  • Label: Equipment ID
  • Static: NO
  • Description: Identifier for the equipment
  • Max Length: 32

After clicking on Add, the new parameter should be added to the parameter list.

HTTP GET Request for retrieving equipment information by ID

 

 

Next, the operation requires an output. Otherwise, the received content could not be used within the process. Therefore, switch to the tab Output and click on Edit List below the dropdown Choose Output. Within the appeared tile click on Add New Output and provide the name 200. Click on Save afterwards. Now, you should be able to create an output format via the Add button on the table with the Body header. This definition will be used as Data Type within the process the action is used.

For most of the cases, the definition of an output schema via the option Fields from Sample JSON is sufficient.  It allows to copy and paste the API response in JSON format from the actual endpoint and use it as blueprint for the resulting types. Apart from the minimisation of overhead resulting from the manual creation of the respective fields, this options also reduces potential mapping errors configuring large response bodies.

Using this approach, the creation of other requests as part of such a library works in a similar way. E.g. the creation of POST or PUT requests requires, in contrast to the created GET request, the additional configuration of a request body (in case you want to send data).

Good to know: To use the Action as part of the modelled process, the library must be released and published. Therefore, click on the Release button in the top right corner. Afterwards select the released version from the dropdown on the top left of the screen. By changing to the version, which is already released, the release button should change to Publish. After clicking on Publish the Action should be available for consumption in the processes within SAP BPA.

Action libraries linked to the implemented process

Automation Steps

Due to the fact, an Action only allows the usage of a static endpoint (defined in the Destination Variables in SAP BPA) and lacks the capability to pre- and post-process the transmitted data, an Automation could be used instead.

Prerequisites: To use this type of process step, an agent must be installed and configured in the SAP BPA instance, and at least the Core SDK must be installed in the process.

Similar to a sub-process step inside a parent process, the Automation allows the business modeller to create standalone reusable processes, which are executed by an agent. One example could be the calling of an API, which is briefly explained as part of this subsection.

For this particular case, the Automation should process the following steps:

  1. Custom script: Prepare data as a JSON body object and pass it down the process chain
  2. Call Web Service: Utilise the JSON object, call a web service API and pass the HTTP response down the process chain
  3. Custom script: Grab the response body from the previous step and return it as output from the Automation
  4. Wait: Wait a certain amount of time to continue

To implement the desired behaviour, the process directly starts with a Custom script control (1). This type of controls offer the possibility to craft functions, written in the programming language JavaScript, to process control inputs and the outputs accordingly. In order to pass values in and out of the controls, they must be defined as part of the Input Parameters and the Output Parameters of the control. This could be done by using the details view of the control after drag-and-drop'ing it into the modelling screen.

An example of creating a custom JavaScript object, which will be passed to the subsequent Call Web Service Activity is shown below.

 

var body = { ... };

return {                    // returns the object as output of the step
    url: 'URL',             // endpoint
    method: 'POST',         // specify HTTP method
    responseType: 'json',   // indicate response type
    resolveBodyOnly: true,  // resolve only the body, not the header values
    headers: {
        'key': 'value'      // header values
    },
    json: body              // to send body as JSON object and not as escaped JSON string
};

 

Good to know: During the process of creating this Automation, the Call Web Service with Destination Activity lacked the behaviour of adding header values to the corresponding requests. Therefore, the headers are added manually in the basic Call Web Service Activity.

The utilised syntax for the created JavaScript object is special to the Call Web Service Activity (2) and can be used directly by the activity without any further processing steps. Only make sure to reference the output of the Custom script control within the Activity. Otherwise, the web service will be called without the prepared parameters. Nevertheless, you could use this object by its name within all steps/Tools in the Automation.

The subsequent step to post-process the response body of the API call (3) works in the exact similar way like the Custom Script implemented at the beginning. So the utilised JavaScript code to map the response into another format will be omitted here. The final step to wait a certain amount of time (4) in order to proceed with the overall process is self-explanatory.

Process Deployment

Finally, the process must be released and deployed. Otherwise, SAP BNAC won't be able detect the process within the Manage Workflows application and no event type could be linked to the process. Therefore, make sure to save all artefacts referenced by the overall process and click on the Release button. During releasing the process, SAP BPA asks the modelling expert to select the Destination Variables, which should be used as endpoints for the Action steps. Therefore, the already configured Destinations should be selected. Afterwards, the released process version is available for deployment in the environments configured within SAP BPA.

To deploy the newest process version, similar to the publishing steps for an Action, select the released version from the dropdown in the middle of the page (tagged with the Release tag) and click on the Deploy button. After a short processing time, you should find your process version deployed in your default environment in SAP BPA.

Deployed process version after releasing

Link Event Type to Process

After the process is published and deployed, the SAP BNAC user is capable of configuring the automation of workflows vie the Manage Workflows application as part of the dashboard. To create a new mapping of an event type to a process, click on the New button in the top right corner of the screen and select the desired event type. After the selection of the event, a deployed process could be selected. Therefore, click on the respective input field and choose the process you want to map. 

Select event typeSelect SAP BPA process for mapping

entry.title: Re: Elevate Your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Architect for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi @PV4SAP 
Thank you for your engagement and providing feedback and posting this very important question.
I would like to encourage rather than posting a question to the blog to us our SAP Learning Q&A  forum to post your question there.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses

Would you mind to repost your question regarding are there any dedicated community / forum there?
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: How do I submit issues, error corrections, comments when I see outdated/wrong info in a learning?
entry.content:
This question is about https://learning.sap.com learning journeys.

For example, learning journeys incorrectly use the term "SAP Cloud Platform" instead of "SAP BTP".

The feedback button on learning journeys asks lots of irrelevant questions. I just want to leave a comment, issue, submit a fix, so that the mistakes can be rectified in future updates. How should I do this? Is there a product update for learning.sap.com in the roadmap?

I have seen information in learning journeys that has been outdated and wrong for >5 years. I think this situation will not improve unless learners are empowered to submit improvements, annotations, or comments.

entry.title: Re: Error Message in ControlCenter DB Instances and Compa...
entry.content:
Hello,

One of the causes might be the disk saturation where your backups are saved. Try to free enough space and your SBO should works again.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: can we use concatenate in where clause of a select query
entry.content:
Hi Poonam,

if you have matching keys in both of table you should avoid using for all entries either should you join.

how ever the suggested appraoch you can use for the solution but i would like to correct a little on this.

instead creating temp field in ekbe prapare a range table and that you can use in where clause no need to use for all entries.

like:

types:ty_combined_value type range of awkey.

data(combined_value) = value ty_combined_value( for ls_ekbe in i_ekbe 

                                                                                 sign = if_fsbp_const_range=>sign_include

                                                                                 option = if_fsbp_const_range=>option_equal

                                                                                 low =  ls_ekbe-belnr &&  ls_ekbe-gjahr ).

 

select * from BKPF into itab

where awkey in combined_value.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Consume local OData Service within same backend system
entry.content:
Wouldn't it be much easier to use RAP EML artifacts to I retract with the rap BOs? https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/examples-for-accessing-business-objects-with-eml?locale=en-US

Cheers,

Frank
